title: cargo-fresh JSON report
description: Schema for the single JSON object emitted to stdout when cargo-fresh runs with --format=json. Stability: schema_version=1 is the 1.0 contract — within 1.x, fields are only added (never removed or renamed). Status lines, prompts, and progress all go to stderr; stdout contains exactly one JSON object per invocation.

schema_version.description: Schema major version. Bumps only on a breaking change; 1.x is additive-only.

format.description: Format discriminator. Lets downstream scripts pin a known shape without parsing semver.

include_prerelease.description: Whether --include-prerelease was active for this run.

dry_run.description: Whether --dry-run was active. If true, `results` reflects what would have happened, not what did.

registry_url.description: Resolved sparse-index base URL used for the run, or null when the default (https://index.crates.io) was used.

updates_available.description: Every package with at least one update candidate found, regardless of whether it was selected for install.

fresh.description: Names of packages already at their latest applicable version.

skipped.description: Packages whose version check was skipped (non-crates source or unknown registry).

results.description: Outcomes of actual `cargo install` invocations. Empty when no updates were applied (e.g. dry-run still records each attempt; --no-interactive with no selection yields an empty array).

aborted.description: True when the run was interrupted (Ctrl-C / SIGINT). Combine with exit code 130 to detect partial runs.

current.description: Installed version, or null if `cargo install --list` did not report one.

prerelease.description: True when `latest` is a SemVer prerelease (only possible with --include-prerelease).

reason.description: Human-readable reason. Stable enough to log; not stable enough to branch on — match on `source` instead.

new_version.description: Version observed after the install attempt. May equal `old_version` if the update ran but the binary didn't actually change.

duration_ms.description: Total wall-clock time from start of run to JSON emission.

sourceKind.description: Short tag derived from PackageSource. `crates` = crates.io / mirror, `git` = git+URL[#rev], `path` = local path, `unknown` = a registry cargo-fresh doesn't speak.
